description:
bl create currently prints NOTHING on success — mutate::run seals and returns Ok(()), and lib::run() exits 0 silently. The skill/contract says 'bl create Prints the new task id to stdout', and callers want id=$(bl create ...); today the minted id is generated inside change::Create (mutate.rs base_change via IdScheme::generate) and never surfaced — only recoverable post-seal from the bl-id trailer or by diffing tasks/. bl-35f7 (main c6b9569) wired execution but deliberately scoped OUT output rendering.

Scope: after a successful seal, emit the result. For create, the new id to stdout (the one machine-relevant fact, scriptable). For the other mutating verbs (claim/unclaim/update/close/drop) a terse human confirmation suffices. The id is the POST-SEAL bl-id trailer (handles a create/pre id reassignment correctly): Engine::seal returns the sealed commit sha; parse the id from its message (message::parse -> bl-id), NOT the pre-seal IdScheme output. Keep stdout the SOLE machine channel — id alone on create so $(...) capture is clean; human confirmations to stderr or suppressible. A --json shape can fold in here or defer to the read-verb rendering ball bl-20fc (decide: shared renderer vs per-class).

Why it matters: without this, create is unusable in a script and gives a human no feedback. It is the missing half of dispatch — execution works, but the result is invisible.
title: §9 create emits the minted id to stdout (+ verb result lines)
